LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PATRICIA M. ORLANDO'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (C.C.P. 425.16). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS SLANDER ARISING FROM DEFENDANT'S COMMUNICATIONS WITH HER SON'S PSYCHOLOGIST AND PEDIATRICIAN. THE COMMUNICATIONS WERE MADE DURING EXAMINATIONS PERFORMED FOR THE PURPOSE OF SEEKING ADVICE AND RECOMMENDATIONS FOR USE IN THE FAMILY COURT PROCEEDINGS. ALL OF THE COMMUNICATIONS ARE CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED SPEECH UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16(E)(2). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING AT TRIAL. DEFENDANT'S STATEMENTS ARE PRIVILEDGED UNDER CC SECTION 47(B)(4), THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE, BECAUSE THEY WERE MADE IN THE INITIATION OR COURSE OF A FAMILY COURT PROCEEDING. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT DEFENDANT PIEL, M.D., MARY RECOVER FROM PLAINTIFF THOMAS, WILLIAM $4,976.50 ATTORNEY FEES, $450.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $5,426.50, SEE SCANNED DOC

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN VIEW OF RULING ON SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANT PIEL ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16. DEFENDANT'S LETTER TO THE COURT IS CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED SPEECH UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16(E). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW HE WOULD PREVAIL AT TRIAL. A DUTY OF CARE DOES NOT EXTEND TO PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT PIEL'S LETTER TO THE FAMILY LAW COURT IS ABSOLUTELY PRIVILEGED UNDER CC SECTION 47(B). DEFENDANT IS AWARDED REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $4976.50 AND $450 IN COSTS AS THE PREVAILING PARTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT PATRICIA M. ORLANDO'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (C.C.P. 425.16).

APPEARANCES: Howard Olsen, Esq. 418-8679 for pltf & opposing party; Patricia Orlando, 474-1418 deft & moving party in propria persona.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT IS SLANDER ARISING FROM DEFENDANT'S COMMUNICATIONS WITH HER SON'S PSYCHOLOGIST AND PEDIATRICIAN. THE COMMUNICATIONS WERE MADE DURING EXAMINATIONS PERFORMED FOR THE PURPOSE OF SEEKING ADVICE AND RECOMMENDATIONS FOR USE IN THE FAMILY COURT PROCEEDINGS. ALL OF THE COMMUNICATIONS ARE CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED SPEECH UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16(e)(2). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING AT TRIAL. DEFENDANT'S STATEMENTS ARE PRIVILEDGED UNDER CC SECTION 47(b)(4), THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE, BECAUSE THEY WERE MADE IN THE INITIATION OR COURSE OF A FAMILY COURT PROCEEDING. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike By Deft
Off Calendar Jul-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARY PIEL, M.D.'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. Appearances: William Thomas (548.1404) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona; Renee A. Richards of Hassard Bonnington LLP (288.9800) appearing for defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: Granted. Plaintiff's claim against defendant Piel arises from protected activity under CCP section 425.16. Defendant's letter to the Court is constitutionally protected speech under CCP section 425.16(e). Plaintiff fails to show he would prevail at trial. A duty of care does not extend to plaintiff. Defendant Piel's letter to the Family Law Court is absolutely privileged under CC section 47(b). Defendant is awarded reasonable attorney fees in the amount of $4976.50 and $450 in costs as the prevailing party. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
